appliedUpdates.description: Added WorkOS device flow and browser flow OAuth endpoint documentation (authorize/device, authorize browser URL, code exchange) to factory-api.md. Previously only the refresh token endpoint was documented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about unauthenticated route allowlist: When adding new OAuth callback routes (e.g., /auth/factory/callback), workers must also add the path to the allowUnauthenticatedRoute check in app.ts. This is not obvious from the existing code and could be missed by future workers adding new provider OAuth integrations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature factory-oauth-routes correctly added /auth/factory/callback to the allowlist in app.ts (line 501-502), following the existing /auth/callback pattern for OpenAI, but the requirement to do this is not documented anywhere.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In the feature-worker skill, for the 'Write Tests First (Red)' step, add a note that for purely frontend/UI features in codebases without React testing infrastructure (no @testing-library/react, no jsdom), agent-browser interactive verification replaces unit tests. Worker should still verify via pnpm typecheck and pnpm test (existing tests pass).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature factory-ui-integration worker could not write meaningful unit tests for React UI components because the codebase has no React testing setup. Worker verified via 5 agent-browser interactive checks instead. This is a reasonable approach but deviates from the skill's test-first step.

rejectedObservations.observation: Factory browser OAuth popup-close handler omits refreshQuota() call that OpenAI equivalent includes, creating an asymmetry that should be documented as a convention.
rejectedObservations.reason: Too specific to be a general convention. The omission is intentional — Factory has no Codex quota system. If Factory adds quota support in the future, it would be a new feature, not a convention gap.
